posts.text: One lesson of the success of GE’s heirs is obvious: businesses perform better when they are not subsumed within giant conglomerates https://www.economist.com/business/2026/03/04/what-the-heirs-to-general-electric-did-next?taid=69ae08d8e9e7b00001532cda&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text:
BREAKING: With US oil prices now up over +$55/barrel in 3 months, rising +100%, our now models indicate US GDP growth will decline by -0.5% if current levels are sustained.

That’s equivalent to roughly -$160 billion less economic output.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text:
The evolution of robotics is bio mimicry.  Nature an incredible engineer.  

I foresee a time when robot parts also function as prosthetics.  The line between human and machine will blur as the two merge in hybrid designs. 

Humans replacing parts and robots adding human lab grown cells. 

Scary and wild at the same time.
posts.author: BambroughKevin
